rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA RUTH GONZALES-LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/06/2021 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A certified copy of letters issued to Vicki Esparza must be filed. Letters must be certified within 60 days. LRSF 14.67(I). Certified letters containing the clerk's original signature and court seal must be filed conventionally with the clerk's office and may not be e-filed. LRSF 14.93(E)(6). 2) Paragraph 18 of the petition and supplement allege that the costs of $2,157.50 were included in the account, but Schedule C shows only two payments to the attorneys' firm on 07/02/2019 & 04/21/2020 totaling $1,360.50. 3) Paragraph 20b requests extraordinary compensation for services related to the petition for determination of entitlement, but this petition was to confirm distribution to Gladys Barros as her trust property. How did this petition benefit the estate and other heirs? 4) Anna Smith's acknowledgment of receipt of service (filed 11/04/2020) was dated 01/02/2020, prior to the date of the filing of this petition. 5) Was the estate the designated beneficiary of the decedent's 457(b) plan benefits? 6) The inventory and appraisal shows only 20 social security checks, but according to Exhibit H, 22 checks appear to have existed. 7) Items 33-37 of Schedule of Property on Hand at Beginning show bank accounts not listed on the inventory and appraisal. Did the decedent own these accounts at the time of her death? 8) Schedule A does not show any receipts between the date of death and 06/23/2017. 9) Schedule A-6 shows only one rent payment on 06/23/2017. When did the tenant vacate? 10) Please explain the nature of the "deposit" on 12/11/2017 reported on Schedule A-6. 11) Please itemize $657.50 "Admin costs" on 08/16/2017 reported on Schedule C-10. 12) The payee and purpose of disbursements on 01/25/2019, 05/07/2019, 10/21/2019, and 10/28/2019 reported on Schedule C-12 are blank. 13) Schedule D-14 & 15 reports complete losses from 12 uncashed checks. Please explain why those checks were not cashed. 14) Schedule D-16 reports a $234 loss of social security benefits. Please explain why the claimed benefits were not fully paid. 15) Schedule E shows funds exceeding the FDIC limit are held by a single financial institution. Please provide proof that the excess funds have been segregated and are currently held by an additional institution. 16) Schedule E shows stocks and bonds with the same beginning carry values, even though the number of shares has changed. 17) Original account statements for all the assets listed on Schedule E must be filed in accordance with LRSF 14.62 & 14.93(D) (If e-filed, a declaration re the retention of original statements must be filed). 18) Schedule F reports distributions and expenses of SFDCP funds. Were the funds sold? If so, each transaction must be reported as a gain or loss, and selling expenses must be reported as disbursements. 19) Re Exhibit F: i) Please identify the hearing dates for the 3 CourtCall appearance invoices paid on 01/27/2020, 02/25/2020, and 03/10/2020. ii) Please provide a copy of a receipt for the $137.85 cost paid on 01/10/2020. 20) Re Exhibit G: Descriptions of services are incomplete. Please add more specifics.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN EVELYN LESLIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/11/21. (No earlier availability.) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.6 A.]. Proposed orders must be provided in hard copy, not merely E-filed. Original bank statements must be filed to verify the cash on hand. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.62.] A declaration must be filed by the attorney or the fiduciary (whoever will retain the originals) re the retention of original documents, which declaration must include all the statements required by LRSF 2.11 M.4. The administrator with will annexed never petitioned for an order determining the parties entitled to distribution, as required by LRSF 14.30. On the other hand, no bank accounts were marshaled (none on the I&A), but the administrator with will annexed proposes to distribute the proceeds from the sale of the tangible personal property to those individuals who would have been entitled to the bank accounts, except those individuals purportedly deleted by the testator. The will was silent regarding disposition of tangible personal property (or any residue).

Court appointed counsel for Conservatee filed a Declaration saying I do not object to the Petition but requests that item #4 in the Order Appointing Conservator "The conservatee's residence shall not be changed from her home located at 535 -42nd Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94121 without court order." 3) Account dates were not set in the related conservatorship order approving account #1.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBERT SUM AND ROSE SUM REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum V. Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner is correct that this motion to quash is untimely per CCP section 1985.3(g), as it was not served prior to the date of production listed on the subpoena. However, the court does not lose jurisdiction to consider a motion to quash that is brought after the subpoena production date. (Slagle v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 211 Cal. App. 3d 1309, 1312). Respondents' motion to quash Petitioner's subpoena for production of business records served on Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical pursuant to CCP section 1987.1 is DENIED. Respondents lack standing to object to the subpoenaed document production under 45 C.F.R. section 164.502(g)(4). Under 45 CFR section 164.502(g)(4): Implementation specification: Deceased individuals. If under applicable law an executor, administrator, or other person has authority to act on behalf of a deceased individual or of the individual's estate, a covered entity must treat such person as a personal representative under this subchapter, with respect to protected health information relevant to such personal representation. Based on the documents filed with the Court, there is no showing that either of the Respondents are decedent's executor, administrator, or "or other person [who] has authority to act on behalf of a deceased individual or of the individual's estate." Petitioner was named as the successor trustee in the Trust instrument executed by decedent and her husband in 1992, with Respondent Charlene Sum named as further successor trustee. (Exh. A of Petitioner's Petition, at Art. 7). Respondents are named as co-trustees in the 2017 Restatement of the Trust, along with decedent. (Exh. A to Respondents' Petition, at Art. 16). Petitioner is named as executor in decedent's 1992 Will, which does not name a successor executor. (Exh. C of Petitioner's Petition, at Art. 4). Neither party makes reference to decedent's 1992 Will having been revised or revoked, either in the motion papers or the underlying petitions and responses. Neither party asserts that decedent appointed a power of attorney for healthcare or as a personal representative for the purposes of 45 C.F.R. section 164.502 or HIPPA generally. Respondents do not offer authority showing that being successor co-trustees of the Revised Trust to which decedent was a settlor and lifetime beneficiary makes them otherwise legally authorized to act on behalf of her estate. Petitioner's evidentiary objection is OVERRULED. Pursuant to CCP section 1987.2(a), sanctions in the amount of $1,200 are awarded to Petitioner and against Respondents, which the Court finds to be a reasonable amount incurred in opposing this motion, including reasonable attorney's fees. Per the findings above, the motion was brought without substantial justification. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 8, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.6 A.]. The petition caption is incomplete, as the petitioners are seeking IAEA authority for the proposed executor. Bond will be required for a non-resident personal representative even if the will waives bond. A declaration must be filed, setting forth the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of bond. [LRSF 14.37.] (No acceptance has been filed by the proposed executor, but it was noted that she signed the Duties and Liabilities. Is she represented by counsel?) The copy of the will attached to the petition is inappropriate; each page of the will must be copied and attached separately. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Item 8 of the petition is incomplete: the relationship to the decedent of the persons listed is not shown. The trustee of the devisee trust must be clearly identified. If the trustee is the same person as the proposed executor, all beneficiaries of the trust must be listed, clearly identified as such, and must be given notice. The petitioners are reportedly "named in decedent's trust;" but that does not give them standing to petition. Are they heirs-at-law or trust beneficiaries? If Patricia Callahan has standing as the executor of the will of Edward Callahan, who is Edward Callahan? A copy of Letters issued to Patricia Callahan must be filed. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] Since the petitioners allege that the decedent was survived by issue of deceased parents, who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? The pleading form of proof of service is inappropriate; the correct proof of service is the one on the reverse side of the mandatory Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, it must include the exact language as on the Judicial Council form Proof of Service and a copy of the Notice that was served must be attached. If it appears that property may pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice must be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of that foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/11/21.
